column1: # REPLACE: Define your protocol's CSV column schema here.
#          Delete all comment lines (lines starting with #) before deploying —
#          standard CSV parsers do not support comments. These lines are here
#          only as documentation in the template.
#   This file is the tag database. Each row defines one data point (tag) that
#   the adapter should read from a field device on every poll cycle.
#   csvparser.py reads this file and merges it with sys_parameters.json to
#   produce the hierarchical poll config consumed by app.py.
# REQUIRED COLUMNS (all protocols must include these):
#   device   — Device identifier string. Must be unique per physical device.
#              Becomes the "device" field in the posted payload.
#   server   — Server/host address used to match this tag to a polling[]
#              entry in sys_parameters.json. Must match exactly.
#              For Modbus TCP / S7: use the IP address (e.g. 192.168.1.100)
#              For OPC UA: use the full server URL (e.g. opc.tcp://192.168.1.100:4840)
#              For Modbus Serial: use the serial port (e.g. /dev/ttyS0)
#   lograte  — Poll interval in milliseconds. Tags with the same lograte are
#              grouped into one poll cycle. Use consistent values.
#   Add all other columns your protocol needs. See existing adapters for reference:
#     address     — OPC UA node ID (e.g. ns=2;s=Tag1)
#     tag         — human-readable tag name posted in the payload
#     isarray     — true/false: whether the node is an array
#     len         — number of registers to read (1=16-bit
#     address     — S7 memory area + address (e.g. DB51
#     offset      — byte offset within the DB or area
# REPLACE: Replace the header row below and the sample rows with your actual schema.
